questions.question: Which of the following is true concerning the production of human insulin in E. coli?

options.A: The A and B chains are produced separately in E. coli and then extracted and linked in vitro.
options.B: The complete proinsulin is synthesized and cleaved inside E. coli.
options.C: E. coli directly secretes fully formed, active insulin.
options.D: E. coli cannot produce human insulin proteins.

questions.question: Myelin sheath in CNS is formed by: (Exam form 934)
questions.solution: By definition, Oligodendrocytes performs this role.

questions.question: Why is Taq polymerase essential for the Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)?

options.A: It is thermostable and does not denature during the high-temperature denaturation phase.
options.B: It works much faster than any human polymerase.
options.D: It does not require template DNA.

questions.question: Which vector is most suitable for cloning the largest fragments of human DNA, such as during the Human Genome Project?

options.A: YACs (Yeast Artificial Chromosomes) and BACs (Bacterial Artificial Chromosomes)

questions.question: The largest gland in the human body is: (Exam form 74)
questions.solution: By definition, Liver performs this role.

questions.question: Which process describes the natural ability of agrobacterium to transfer T-DNA into the plant genome?
What is the purpose of 'insertional inactivation' in genetic engineering?

options.A: To screen and separate recombinant colonies from non-recombinant colonies
options.B: To destroy the host cell's DNA
options.C: To activate the toxic genes of a pathogen
options.D: To stop the replication of the plasmid completely

questions.question: Which ethical argument is most frequently raised against the patenting of indigenous plant varieties by foreign multinational corporations?

options.A: It ignores the traditional knowledge and prior breeding efforts of local farmers (Biopiracy).
options.B: It leads to the extinction of the plant.
options.C: The patents are always scientifically invalid.
options.D: It violates the laws of thermodynamics.
